title: Balikatan live-fire drills feature US Marines at Subic Bay beaches

contents:
# Balikatan live-fire drills feature US Marines at Subic Bay beaches

## Overview
US Marine Corps and Philippine Marines conducted amphibious live-fire exercise on Subic Bay shores as part of Balikatan. HIMARS rockets and artillery targeted mock positions. Exercise emphasized rapid deployment tactics.

## Details
Involved 3rd Marine Regiment from Hawaii and Philippine Marine Brigade. Fired 12 HIMARS rounds plus 155mm howitzers. No casualties; all munitions accounted for. Joint Task Force commander Col. Michael James stated "precision strikes enhance deterrence." Drones provided overwatch. Olongapo City provided emergency support standby.

**Location**: Subic Bay Freeport Zone, Philippines

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @olongapotimes, which covers Olongapo Subic Bay US Navy Philippines port calls Balikatan. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Olongapo Subic Bay US Navy Philippines port calls Balikatan will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
